gaps.title: Catalogue-gate false-rejects DB-enumerated entities (completeness + variant tolerance + school short form)
gaps.description: The free-text catalogue-gate (planner/_freetext_audit.py CatalogueUnion/build_catalogue_union/admits) false-rejects values that ARE enumerated in the school DB; it must reject only genuine fabrications. Three modes (Explore live-probe, current-source-verified): (a) COMPLETENESS - framing_vocabularies and priority_tiers are emitted by build_grounding (SECTION_KEYS, builders grounding.py:111/:118) but absent from the union src tuple (_freetext_audit.py:653-668), so their labels (e.g. Student-focused, High) are false-rejected; both models live (school/models/framing.py:26, priority_tier.py:23, no delete migration). (b) VARIANT - admits is exact union_casefold membership (+ -es/-s plural strip) with no hyphen/space/underscore/case collapse, so any formatting variant of any enumerated entity is false-rejected. (c) SHORT FORM - the school brand stem (Chiway-Repton, a token-prefix of Chiway Repton School Xiamen) is false-rejected. Fix (global, pure code, no migration, planner/_freetext_audit.py only): pull the two sections into the union; add a _collapse_normalize helper + a precomputed union_collapsed set (every entry collapse + every >=2-token contiguous prefix of the school name/slug); admits gains one union_collapsed membership check. Preserve fabrication rejection (a name whose collapse matches no entry/prefix still rejects). DEC-45 stands: MissionArea deleted, Caring not enumerated and correctly rejected; no sub-token matching. Surfaced in FEAT-002 apply-smoke; user directive: think globally, no enumerated-from-DB entity is rejected.

evidence.reference: school-improvement-plans/planner/_freetext_audit.py:529-551 (admits); :653-668 (union src tuple); :486-488 (_normalize_for_catalogue)

gaps.impact: DB-enumerated entities (the school name + its short form, framing_vocabularies, priority_tiers, and any catalogued value's formatting variant) were false-rejected as fabrications, blocking legitimate prose in the create-plan draft.
gaps.proposed_resolution: Resolved (TASK-013 / VER-003): framing_vocabularies + priority_tiers pulled into the union + PhraseMatcher; a _collapse_normalize helper + a precomputed union_collapsed (every entry collapse + every >=2-token contiguous prefix of the school name/slug); admits gains one union_collapsed membership check. Fabrications still reject. Committed on main.

gaps.title: FLAG-MODEL A1: surface DEC-44 verdicts (catalogue-variant/ordinary-prose), not just fabricated
gaps.description: The DEC-44 LLM fabrication validator (planner/_llm_fabrication_validator.py:83 validate_candidates) already classifies each candidate proper-name span into fabricated | catalogue-variant | ordinary-prose (the system prompt defines all three at :68 and :198), but :144 filters the return to verdict==fabricated only, discarding catalogue-variant and ordinary-prose. The three-valued validation model needs the verdict propagated: catalogue-variant -> FLAG (admit-with-annotation, not reject), ordinary-prose -> admit, fabricated -> reject-or-flag. Change validate_candidates to return (span, verdict) pairs so callers can route by verdict. Verified live + wired (called from _freetext_audit.py:972). Atomic gap in the three-valued (accept/flag/hard-reject) flagging model.

evidence.reference: school-improvement-plans/planner/_llm_fabrication_validator.py:83-145

gaps.impact: The validator already distinguishes catalogue-variant and ordinary-prose from fabricated, but those verdicts are discarded — so a legitimate paraphrase/variant cannot be flagged-and-admitted, and the three-valued model has no verdict to route on.
gaps.proposed_resolution: Change validate_candidates to return (span, verdict) pairs so callers route catalogue-variant -> flag, ordinary-prose -> admit, fabricated -> reject-or-flag.

gaps.title: FLAG-MODEL A2: audit_prefill returns flags for catalogue-survivors instead of raising; structural/FK/invariant stay hard-reject
gaps.description: audit_prefill (planner/_freetext_audit.py:1320-1338) currently raises ValueError on the first non-empty free-text survivors list, which the parsers propagate as a hard reject (apply exits non-zero, workflow halts). The three-valued model needs this single class - free-text proper-name survivors from the catalogue gate - to become a FLAG (admit-with-annotation) rather than a hard reject. audit_prefill should return a flags list (path, span, verdict from A1, reason) instead of raising. This is the load-bearing HARD/SOFT PARTITION: ONLY free-text catalogue-survivors become flags; every structural failure (unparseable JSON, missing/extra keys, wrong types, gates), coded-FK failure (improvement_type/planning_method/division/position/standard/frequency/timing/kind/enum), index/range check, and cross-field invariant (type-method, per-kind date rules, target-iff-kind, verbatim statement match) STAYS a hard reject (data unmergeable). FREETEXT_SELECTORS_BY_SPEC (:1301) already scopes which 12 of 14 specs/fields are free-text (flag-eligible); B2/F2 have none. Atomic gap; depends on A1.

evidence.reference: school-improvement-plans/planner/_freetext_audit.py:1301-1338

gaps.impact: audit_prefill raises on the first free-text survivor, so a single catalogue-survivor hard-halts the whole apply/sequence instead of becoming a reviewable flag.
gaps.proposed_resolution: audit_prefill returns a flags list (path, span, verdict, reason) for free-text catalogue-survivors; structural/FK/invariant/index failures stay hard-reject. FREETEXT_SELECTORS_BY_SPEC scopes the 12 flag-eligible specs.

gaps.title: FLAG-MODEL A3: AssistResult gains a flags field; the 12 free-text parsers populate it
gaps.description: AssistResult (planner/assist.py:33-57) carries only prefill + optional note. Add an optional flags field (list of {path, span, verdict, severity, message}). The 12 free-text parsers in planner/specs.py (all except B2 bind-measurement-channels and F2 propose-assignments, per FREETEXT_SELECTORS_BY_SPEC) currently call audit_prefill expecting it to raise; they must instead collect the flags it returns (A2) and attach them to the returned AssistResult, while still raising on the hard-reject classes. parse_narrative (A1) returns a bare dict today, so the narrative parser also needs the AssistResult(+flags) shape or an equivalent carrier. Bare-return parsers normalize through views.py / apply, so a flags channel must thread there too (see C2/B2). Atomic gap; depends on A2.

evidence.reference: school-improvement-plans/planner/assist.py:33-57; planner/specs.py (12 audit_prefill call sites)

gaps.impact: AssistResult cannot carry flags, so the parsers have nowhere to return soft-flag annotations to the apply / UX layers.
gaps.proposed_resolution: Add an optional flags field to AssistResult; the 12 free-text parsers collect audit_prefill flags and attach them (raising only on hard-reject classes); the bare-return narrative parser gains the same carrier.

gaps.title: FLAG-MODEL B2: apply records flags + exits 0 (not 1) on soft-flag; capture/result envelope carry flags
gaps.description: Both apply paths hard-halt on the parser today: apply.py:295-300 and apply_from_substrate.py:113-121 catch any parse exception and return 1 (workflow halts at that step; run_workflow.mjs:478 treats exit 1 as halt, exit 0 as continue). For a soft-flag outcome (A2/A3 - the parser returned prefill + flags, no hard raise), apply must: merge the prefill, record the flags into the draft flags channel (B1), set the capture/envelope validation status to flagged (apply.py capture :346-361 hardcodes schema_validation:passed; apply_from_substrate result envelope :143-152 has status:merged), include the flags in the emitted JSON, and EXIT 0 so the 14-spec sequence runs to completion accumulating flags. Hard rejects still exit 1 (halt). THIS IS THE ACCELERATION: the sequence completes in one pass producing a whole draft + a flag batch, instead of halting at the first catalogue-survivor. Atomic gap; depends on A3, B1.

evidence.reference: school-improvement-plans/prompt-workshop/dispatch/apply.py:295-361; apply_from_substrate.py:113-152; run_workflow.mjs:478

gaps.impact: Both apply paths exit non-zero on any parser raise, halting the sequence at the first catalogue-survivor — preventing a complete draft from one pass.
gaps.proposed_resolution: On a soft-flag return (prefill + flags), merge, record flags into the draft channel, mark the capture/envelope flagged, emit flags, and exit 0 (hard rejects still exit 1) so the sequence completes accumulating flags.

gaps.title: FLAG-MODEL C1: a Flag model on Plan (net-new) to persist human-review annotations
gaps.description: There is NO flag/annotation model in production (verified: no class Flag in plans/models/; ReviewEvent/ReviewEventInput/RevisionRule are the plans built-in review loop, and ActionStepImpediment is orthogonal per services.py - none are draft-element validation annotations). This is the production-side persistence the workshop draft flags channel (B1) maps onto when the orchestration trio (US-LLM-23/24/25, currently UNBUILT) lands. Requires a DB migration. Atomic gap; prerequisite for C2/C3.

evidence.reference: school-improvement-plans/plans/models/ (net-new Flag model + migration)

gaps.impact: No production model persists per-element review flags, so flags surfaced at generation cannot be carried to the plan for human resolution.
gaps.proposed_resolution: Net-new Flag model FK to Plan (element_path, severity, message, verdict, is_resolved, created_by_spec, created_at) + migration.

gaps.title: FLAG-MODEL C2: assist response carries flags; client renders inline badges + resolution UX
gaps.description: AssistStreamView (planner/views.py:404) streams SSE; the result frame (:506) normalizes AssistResult to {prefill, note}; the error frame (:508) carries {error}. The client (planner/static/wizard/js/ai-assist.js) applies prefill DIRECTLY on the result frame (no approval gate) and shows event:error in the status line. This is the DISC-30/DISC-31 UX-layer closure (admin sees an actionable signal, not event:error). All net-new. Atomic gap; depends on A3 (+ C1 if resolution persists to the Flag model).

gaps.title: FLAG-MODEL C3: promotion-gate predicate - zero unresolved must-fix flags
gaps.description: _check_promotion_gates (plans/services.py:98-203) aggregates all promotion predicates into a failures list and is invoked by advance_plan_status (:206) only on the transition to Lifecycle.ACTIVE (proposed->active). It currently checks no flags (no Flag model exists). Add a predicate: if the Plan has any Flag with severity=must_fix and is_resolved=False, append a failure (block promotion until resolved/accepted). This is the contract shift the three-valued model implies: enforcement moves from generation-time hard-reject to promotion-time flag-resolution, so the DEC-29 promotion-ready predicate becomes zero-unresolved-must-fix-flags. evidence.reference: school-improvement-plans/plans/services.py:98-225

gaps.title: apply.py mypy debt (un-suppressed): get_parse_function's Callable type omits school/grounding kwargs -> 2 call-arg + 1 unused-ignore; gate RED
gaps.description: The B1 negligence-suppression (pyproject [[tool.mypy.overrides]] module=['dispatch.apply'] ignore_errors=true) has been REMOVED, re-exposing apply.py's real mypy errors to the standing `mypy .`: (1) apply.py:294 `parse_fn(response_text, school=school, grounding=grounding)` produces 2 `call-arg` errors (Unexpected keyword argument 'school' / 'grounding') because `parse_fn = get_parse_function(args.spec_key)` (prompt-workshop/dispatch) returns a Callable whose type does NOT declare the school/grounding keyword params -- although every concrete parser accepts `*, school=None, grounding=None` and it works at runtime (a typing imprecision, not a runtime bug); (2) apply.py:268 carries an unused `# type: ignore`. gaps.title: Gate's pytest runs on SQLite — Postgres-only constraint violations (VARCHAR length, CHECK, UNIQUE) are invisible to the gate
gaps.description: config/settings/test.py runs the suite on in-memory SQLite, which does not enforce VARCHAR max_length, CHECK constraints, or DB-level UNIQUE the way the dev/production Postgres does. A class of bugs therefore passes `pytest` (the standing gate) on SQLite yet raises DataError / IntegrityError on Postgres in production: an ORM create/save writing author-controlled strings into a capped column, a CHECK-violating row, a UNIQUE collision. Surfaced by TASK-020/FGAP-008's _save_flag_requests, where a valid-JSON oversize flag-request value 500s on Postgres but no SQLite test could catch it; TASK-020's fix routes through full_clean() so the ValidationError path (which fires on BOTH DBs) is the safety net.

evidence.reference: test-suite DATABASES = in-memory SQLite (~:22-27); pytest selects this settings module

gaps.impact: The gate's pytest cannot catch Postgres-only constraint violations, so such bugs pass green and fail in production — a latent blind spot for any code path writing unvalidated input to constrained columns.
gaps.proposed_resolution: Mitigation already applied in TASK-020: full_clean() before saving author input + a test asserting the ValidationError path. Fuller fix is a scope call: run the gate's pytest against Postgres (full fidelity) or add a Postgres CI lane. User scopes.

gaps.title: load_draft crashes on an empty --draft-path: Path('')==Path('.') skips the empty-shell fallback -> IsADirectoryError
gaps.description: _workshop.py load_draft(path) does `target = path or draft_path(); if not target.exists(): return empty_draft_shell(); return json.loads(target.read_text())`. An empty --draft-path (argparse type=Path turns '' into Path(''), which equals Path('.'), an existing directory) is truthy, so target=Path('.'), .exists() is True, the empty-shell fallback is skipped, and read_text() on the directory raises IsADirectoryError (exit 1). Surfaced by TASK-010: the workflow interpolates an absent input.draft_path to --draft-path '', so spec-1 (the empty-draft start, criterion 2) crashes at its first render/apply. The fallback works for an omitted flag (path=None) or a nonexistent path, but not empty string. Workflow-driven bridges hit it: render_from_substrate.py:88 and apply_from_substrate.py:104,133 (args.draft_path); render.py:67 also if --draft '' is passed. apply.py/sequence.py/verify-render-parity.py call load_draft() with no path, so are unaffected.

evidence.reference: :255-259 load_draft fallback; target=path or draft_path(); Path('')==Path('.') exists+is_dir -> read_text IsADirectoryError
:104,133 load_draft(args.draft_path)
:88 load_draft(args.draft_path)

gaps.impact: The 14-spec workflow cannot start from the empty draft (TASK-010 criterion 2): spec-1's render + apply crash on the engine-interpolated --draft-path ''. Blocks TASK-010 / FEAT-003.
gaps.proposed_resolution: In load_draft (the shared helper -> fixes every caller), treat an empty path (Path('')/Path('.')) as absent and return a fresh empty_draft_shell() (NOT the draft_path() default, which could load a stale current-draft.json). Preserves: omitted flag -> shell; real existing path -> loads; nonexistent path -> shell; empty -> shell. Add a regression test for the empty-path case.

gaps.title: Workshop prompt render autoescapes HTML-special chars in catalogue values; the agent copies the entity -> exact-match parser hard-rejects
gaps.package: prompt-workshop (render path; + production PromptTemplate.render per DEC-41)
gaps.description: render_from_substrate.py:109 (and the substrate-assembled + verify-render-parity render paths) call Django `Template(body).render(Context(...))` with autoescape ON by default. A catalogue value containing an HTML-special char (apostrophe, &, <, >, ") renders as an entity -- e.g. the division `Principal's Office (PO)` renders as `Principal's Office (PO)` (confirmed in the live render, run ...065027). The agent faithfully copies the entity-bearing token; the parser catalogue check (planner/specs.py:499 `if value in members`) exact-matches the un-escaped catalogue value -> hard reject. Surfaced at spec 6 (propose-assignments, responsible_division) once the FGAP-014 fix let the run reach a catalogue value with an apostrophe. Orthogonal to FGAP-014 (concatenation): these prompts are PLAIN TEXT, not HTML, so Django autoescape is wrong for them. Blast: any emitted catalogue value with an HTML-special char, across all specs (possessive names / apostrophes are common).

evidence.reference: :109 Template(body).render(Context(...)) -- Django autoescape on by default
rendered division line 'Principal's Office' (apostrophe -> ')
:499 _check_in_catalogue exact match; F2 reject responsible_division='Principal's Office (PO)' vs catalogue 'Principal's Office (PO)'

gaps.impact: Blocks the run at any spec whose validly-chosen catalogue value contains an HTML-special char; spec 6 is the first. The DISC-26 promotion-ready draft cannot assemble until fixed.
gaps.proposed_resolution: Render the plain-text prompts with Django autoescape OFF (wrap the body in {% autoescape off %}...{% endautoescape %}, or render via a Template/Engine with autoescape=False), applied at the render call in BOTH the snippet and substrate render paths in lockstep so verify-render-parity stays 14/14 (render_from_substrate.py + the _workshop assemble + verify-render-parity renders). Reject parser entity-tolerance (DEC-40). Production PromptTemplate.render likely shares the bug (parallel; DEC-41 migration-time). The next DISC-26 iteration.

gaps.title: propose-responsibilities cannot run: the responsibility-atom layer is entirely unseeded for the tenant (all divisions + positions have zero atoms)
gaps.package: prompt-workshop (snippet 07 + shared preamble); DISC-30 admin-curation class
gaps.description: At spec 7 (propose-responsibilities, US-LLM-27) the run halts. Querying the Chiway grounding (build_grounding) shows ALL 15 divisions AND all ~46 positions have ZERO responsibility atoms -- the DEC-32 Layer-1 responsibility-atom data is entirely unseeded for this tenant. The parser gates each division-grain and position-grain `statement` verbatim against these (empty) per-owner atom sets (planner/specs.py:543 _responsibility_statements_under_division -> div['responsibilities']; positions analogously), so NO proposal can pass for ANY owner. 'Principal's Office (PO)' was merely the first owner the run reached. The shared preamble's all-divisions mandate pushes the LLM to propose for every division (copying the division description rendered at 07:27/07:29), but there are no atoms to match. Primary root cause: the uncurated responsibility-atom catalogue (DISC-30 admin-curation / tenant-data gap), NOT a prompt tension -- the all-divisions-vs-empty-set tension is moot when every set is empty; the spec simply cannot produce valid output for this tenant until the atoms are curated.

Blocks the run at spec 7. Any tenant whose responsibility-atom layer is unseeded hits this; it is the DISC-30 onboarding-data class, surfaced by the workshop run.

gaps.title: PromptSanitizer rejects legitimate plan prose containing 'system:'/'user:'/'assistant:' — the role-token injection patterns are too broad (production + workshop)
gaps.description: render_suggest_feedback_channels (spec 11) crashed: PromptSanitizationError 'system message injection detected'. Root cause (verified at source): the DATA_INJECTION_PATTERNS role-token regexes are too broad and the sanitizer REJECTS (raises) rather than scrubs. The pattern `(?i)\bsystem\s*:\s*` (prompt_sanitizer.py:44) matched a legitimate evidence-artifact location in the accumulated draft: action_steps[4].evidence_artifacts[1].location = 'PD schedule system: /professional-learning/2025-2026/'. sanitize_data (prompt_sanitizer.py:106) runs DATA_INJECTION_PATTERNS and raises on any match; sanitize_data_dict (:185) applies it indiscriminately to every string in the grounding bundle. BOTH layers hit it: the workshop render (render_from_substrate.py:107 sanitize_data_dict(grounding)) AND production (PromptTemplate.render llm.py:217 sanitize_data_dict(context); AssistStreamView views.py:550-551 tpl.render(**grounding) where grounding includes the author's draft_state). So this is a PRODUCTION bug too -- any plan whose prose legitimately contains 'system:'/'user:'/'assistant:' (e.g. '...reporting system:', 'IT systems:') hard-fails the render. Surfaced now only because FGAP-022 let the run reach spec 11 (the accumulated draft made a benign 'system:' near-certain).

:107 PromptSanitizer.sanitize_data_dict(grounding) over the whole bundle incl. draft_state
:217 PromptTemplate.render sanitize_data_dict(context) -> production has the same false-positive
:526 draft_state=body.get('draft_state') (author's in-progress, untrusted); :550-551 build_grounding+tpl.render
draft-after-propose-evidence.json action_steps[4].evidence_artifacts[1].location = 'PD schedule system: /professional-learning/2025-2026/' (1 benign hit; no user:/assistant: hits)

gaps.impact: The 14-spec run cannot complete (halts at spec 11). Independent of FGAP-022 (whose cap-overflow target IS fixed -- the run reached 30/31, past the heavy specs). In PRODUCTION the same render-reject hard-fails any assist whose grounding/draft prose legitimately contains a role token + colon -- a latent availability bug for real plans.
gaps.proposed_resolution: RECOMMENDED: tighten the role-token patterns (system:/user:/assistant: and the code-block/xml/inst variants if needed) to fire only at a TURN BOUNDARY -- start-of-string or newline-preceded (e.g. `(?im)(?:^|\n)\s*system\s*:`) -- so a real role-injection ('\nsystem: ignore...') still matches but mid-prose 'PD schedule system: /path' does not. Production-shared (DEC-41) -> fixes both layers; excludes NO data -> no trust-boundary weakening. REJECTED: excluding draft_state from the check (the Explore's first instinct) -- in production draft_state is the AUTHOR's untrusted input (views.py:526), so blanket-excluding it would WEAKEN production injection protection. Verify a tightened pattern still catches the existing role-injection test cases; add a regression test for benign 'X system: /path' prose. Pattern change touches a production security layer -- user to confirm the security posture.

gaps.title: PromptSanitizer role-token check is evadable by TOKEN-glyph obfuscation (fullwidth / homoglyph / combining-mark) — distinct from the FGAP-023 gap-evasion class; needs CJK-safe confusable folding
gaps.description:
Surfaced by the FGAP-023 iteration-4 adversarial audit (a DISTINCT class from FGAP-023, which closed benign-prose tolerance + separator/invisible gap-evasion between a boundary and an ASCII token). The role-token patterns match ASCII 'system'/'user'/'assistant'; the render/dispatch path applies NO NFKC/NFC normalization (grep of ai/, planner/, prompt-workshop/dispatch/, the Anthropic provider returns none). So the TOKEN GLYPHS can be obfuscated to evade the regex while a model may still read a role turn: (3a) fullwidth/compatibility -- '
ｓystem: evil' (U+FF53), '
system： evil' (U+FF1A) BYPASS; (3b) homoglyph -- '
ѕystem:' (Cyrillic U+0455), '
systеm:' (U+0435) BYPASS; (3c) in-token combining mark -- '
sy͏stem:' (U+034F CGJ, category Mn -> kept) BYPASS. Credibility is uneven + model-tokenizer-dependent (fullwidth highest; CGJ-split lowest).

evidence.reference: role-token patterns match ASCII tokens; _normalize_separators folds Cf/Zs/line-breaks but does NOT NFKC-normalize the token glyphs
no unicodedata.normalize/NFKC/NFC anywhere (grep ai/ planner/ prompt-workshop/dispatch/ + provider)
fullwidth/homoglyph/CGJ token forms BYPASS sanitize_data + sanitize_refinement; gap-class itself is converged (0 leaks)

gaps.impact: A crafted role-token using non-ASCII glyphs could evade the injection check on both the workshop render and production PromptTemplate.render. Lower-credibility than the gap-evasion class (requires the model's tokenizer to read the obfuscated glyphs as the ASCII role marker) + the actor in production is an authenticated admin editing their own plan. Real but distinct debt, recorded per mandate-007 (user decides scope: address now or as a follow-up).
gaps.proposed_resolution: NFKC-fold (and/or apply a confusables map) the string BEFORE the role-token regex check -- but CAREFULLY: NFKC alters compatibility/fullwidth CJK forms, and this is a bilingual (Chinese) tenant (Chiway), so blanket NFKC of stored/forwarded content could mangle legitimate CJK. Likely shape: NFKC a COPY used only for the injection pattern check (not the content that reaches the LLM), or a targeted confusable-fold limited to the Latin role-token glyphs + strip category-Mn marks for the check only. Needs its own canonical-pipeline pass (explore the CJK-safety + the check-only-copy design). Distinct from FGAP-023.

gaps.title: Tenant responsibility-atom data does not realize the DEC-48 SAO out-of-lesson roles: YGL absent, HRT carries a misplaced in-lesson atom
gaps.description: DEC-48 settles that HRT, YGL, and Dormitory Teacher are PURE SAO roles for planning (targeted only in-role, never as the individual subject teachers who fill them; the in-lesson responsibility belongs to the subject-teacher seat). The tenant data does not realize this: (1) YGL (Year Group Leader) is ABSENT from the responsibility-atom seed entirely, though the Position model supports it (year_group FK explicitly for 'AS/A2/IG1/IG2 Year Leader' + kind=LEADER; YearGroups seeded by migration 0046: IG1/IG2/AS/A2). (2) The Homeroom Teacher seat carries a MISPLACED atom 'Take primary responsibility for developing the school-wide learner outcomes... in every lesson - 100% in-class time' (CSV line ~60) — that is the subject-teacher seat's in-lesson responsibility, not the HRT seat's; per DEC-48 it must be removed so the HRT seat carries only SAO out-of-lesson atoms. (3) Dormitory Teacher is present under SAO with its residential out-of-lesson atom (correct). CONSEQUENCE: the generated plan draft cannot target these out-of-lesson pastoral roles correctly — the distiller-input audit found the draft dropped HRT/Dorm and lacks YGL, so out-of-lesson SLO ownership is unrepresentable. All three must sit under the SAO division carrying the SAO out-of-lesson responsibility set; YGL is per year group.

gaps.title: Responsibility-atom importer is additive-only: removing an atom/row from the CSV does NOT un-seed it from an already-imported DB
gaps.description: The importer is additive + idempotent BY DESIGN (re-runnable materialized-defaults seeder; get_or_create on (owner, statement)). It never deletes. So editing data/chiway-responsibility-atoms-template.csv to REMOVE an atom (or a position's owner row) stops RE-creating it but leaves any previously-imported copy in the DB. Surfaced in FGAP-025/DEC-48: removing the HRT teacher-base atoms from the CSV did not remove them from the already-seeded dev DB; the orchestrator had to delete the 3 stale PositionResponsibility rows directly (a one-time manual correction) to realize the target. The CSV is the source of truth and is now correct, but the importer cannot reconcile the DB to a SHRUNK CSV.

evidence.reference: _attach_position_atom get_or_create on (position, statement_en) — create-or-dedup only; no prune of atoms absent from the CSV
removing the HRT in-lesson atoms from the CSV left them in the dev DB; required a direct orchestrator delete of 3 stale rows

gaps.impact: Any future tenant-data correction that REMOVES an atom/seat via the CSV silently fails to un-seed an already-imported DB — a footgun: the CSV looks authoritative but the DB drifts. Low blast radius today (dev DB, manual correction works), but it makes the CSV non-declarative for removals.
gaps.proposed_resolution: Decide (human scope) whether the importer should gain a RECONCILE mode (delete, per seat, the materialized atoms no longer present in the CSV for that seat) so the CSV becomes fully declarative — bounded carefully (a partial CSV must not delete legit atoms; likely scoped per-owner-touched, or a separate --prune flag). Alternatively keep it additive and document that removals require a manual DB correction (or a fresh re-seed). Distinct from FGAP-025 (which is closed by the data correction + the year_group extension).

gaps.title: Ownership distribution concentrates at the ASSIGNMENT layer because the chosen method's templates are single-purview (one-step-per-template x one-best-fit-assignment)
gaps.description: ROOT CAUSE (verified, see DEC-50): action concentration is architectural. Spec 05 generates the action set by SELECTING ONE planning method and EXPANDING its templates (one-per-template); all 4 catalogued methods are single-purview process cycles; spec 05 does not even receive division_responsibility_atoms; spec 07 only ANNOTATES the method-derived steps. So the responsibility map is downstream annotation, never the upstream driver of actions -> the plan talks school-wide but acts one-division. NOT an assignment-logic miss (spec 06) and NOT fixable by FEAT-006 (can not invent un-generated actions). gaps.title: Transposition regression: whole-draft invariants landed as post-hoc checks only, with no generation-time contract or in-execution evaluation
gaps.package: prompt-workshop (sim->workshopping transposition): shared/preamble.md + snippets/01,02 + check_draft.py + the 14-spec run method
gaps.description: PROCESS ROOT (root-caused by an independent audit): the sim-land -> workshopping transposition moved each sim discipline into the corpus WITHOUT a verification step that it survived as BOTH (a) a generation-time contract AND (b) an in-execution (per-spec, during-run) evaluation. Per-cell/per-step disciplines (R1-R6: register, learning-designed actions, three-view measurement, growth-mindset, quarterly cadence, distribution) landed as generation contracts in the preamble/snippets and are checkable. The WHOLE-DRAFT invariants -- improvement-area coverage above all -- landed as POST-HOC CHECKS ONLY (check_draft Check 4, lines 490-546), with NO generation-time instruction in any spec and NO per-spec self-check; the linear render->apply pipeline has no feedback loop, so an under-covering spec is never re-prompted. RESULT REGRESSION (the trigger): a full 14-spec assembled draft (current-draft.json) covers learner outcomes 7/7 but improvement areas only 3/7 -- 4 silently dropped (Bilingual Environment, Community Involvement, Resources and Tools, Student Data and Well-being), neither targeted in domain_alignment nor mentioned in prose. Mechanism: snippets/02-propose-domain-alignment.md line 57 mandates >=1 area_for_improvement, never all-relevant-or-surface-the-rest-as-gaps; snippets/01-narrative-draft.md lists the areas in grounding but never mandates addressing/acknowledging each; check_draft catches the silent gap only after assembly. SECONDARY regression: in the same assembled run, owner distribution degraded to 3 owners / 50% max (Curriculum and Teaching 3/6) -- passing the gate only at the boundary -- vs the isolated propose-assignments run (VER-031: 5 owners / 33%); the full pipeline concentrates ownership more than the isolated check showed. OPEN (audit could not confirm from source, needs verification): whether the three-view (student/staff/parent) measurement and the learning-designed contact-type action-step shape hold across the assembled draft, or also degraded -- neither has an in-execution or post-hoc gate.

evidence.reference: check_draft --strict: Check 4 coverage FAIL (improvement areas 3/7, 4 SILENT GAPs); Check 3 distribution 3 owners/50% max
line 57: areas_for_improvement requires >=1, no all-or-surface-gap mandate
lines ~40-42 list areas in grounding; no mandate to address/acknowledge each
lines 490-546 coverage is post-hoc only; no generation-time signal, no feedback loop to spec 02

gaps.impact: Generated whole-plan drafts can silently omit improvement areas (and potentially other whole-draft qualities) -- the plan looks complete but under-covers the school's own improvement agenda; regressions are discoverable only at final assembly, not during execution, defeating early correction. This is the class of failure any future tenant's run will hit. Establishes the need to (1) make whole-draft invariants generation-time contracts with per-spec in-execution evaluation, and (2) add a transposition-verification discipline ensuring every sim finding survives as contract + in-execution eval, not check-only.
gaps.proposed_resolution: (1) Coverage as a generation contract: snippet 02 (and/or 01) must account for EVERY improvement area the goal bears on -- target it, or explicitly surface it as a not-this-cycle gap recorded in the draft -- and a per-spec self-check evaluates this during the run, not only at output. (2) Build in-execution evaluation: each spec carries exact success + process criteria the generation step self-verifies before apply (DEC for the principle). (3) Confirm + address the secondary distribution degradation and the open three-view/learning-design questions across the assembled draft. (4) Audit the full transposition map (each sim finding -> generation contract + in-execution eval), filing any other check-only/absent discipline. Execute via the canonical pipeline once scoped.

gaps.title: The originating theme (the `seed`) enters every spec only as a one-line optional steer, not as a structural driver of the plan's organizing dimensions
gaps.package: prompt-workshop generation — the ideation specs 03/04/05 + the seed grounding mechanism; DEC-52 theme-coherence
gaps.description: The author `seed` (the originating theme, e.g. 'Improve SLO usage across the school') enters as a required workflow input and is threaded --seed into ALL 14 render steps, resolved per render and injected into the template context. But in every spec it appears ONLY as a one-line nudge: spec 01 inline; specs 02-14 as the {% if seed %} 'Author refinement (an optional steer for the <task>)' fragment. Corroborated against source: in the IDEATION specs the seed line sits in the grounding preamble and the TASK BODY derives the plan's substance from the CATALOGUES + accumulated draft_state, NOT from the theme — propose-milestones (03) chooses improvement_type CODES from the catalogue + reads draft_state, with no directive to derive the plan's organizing dimensions FROM the seed/theme. The seed is also NOT carried in draft_state (re-injected each render; production-parity). So the theme steers every step but does not structurally organize the plan. DISTINCT from FGAP-030 (the guiding_statements/mission-vision grounding-REACH vector); FGAP-043 is the seed/theme vector. The intended behavior (sim run-006): the framework is theme-agnostic and the organizing axis should be DERIVED FROM the theme.

evidence.reference: seed = required input ('threaded into every spec's render'); --seed passed to all 14 render steps
seed resolved per render and injected into the Django template context

seed appears ONLY as the '{% if seed %}Author refinement (an optional steer for the milestones)' line; the task body derives milestones from the improvement_types catalogue + draft_state, NOT from the theme
spec 01 inline seed: 'Author seed (a one-line intent for this plan): {{ seed }}'
the FRAG-gb-seed-* 'optional steer' fragments, one per spec 02-14
seed NOT carried in draft_state; re-injected each render (production-parity via PromptTemplate.render(seed=))

authoritative intended behavior: framework is THEME-AGNOSTIC; 'the organizing axis is derived FROM the theme … add derive this plan's organizing dimensions from the theme'; this finding is UNFILED (grep-confirmed across framework-gaps/seed-round-plan/discoveries)

gaps.impact: A plan authored with a strong thematic seed is not structurally organized by it: the milestones/criteria/action-steps' organizing dimensions come from the LLM's free inference over the catalogues + draft_state, with the theme only a one-line advisory. The plan may cohere if the LLM infers it, but it is not driven by the theme — violating the sim run-006 intent (organizing axis derived FROM the theme) and the DEC-52 theme-coherence requirement at the ideation layer. Sibling-class to FGAP-030; together they are the 'ideation should be DRIVEN by the input vectors (theme + mission/vision/SLO), not just steered by them' gap.
gaps.proposed_resolution: Per sim run-006: make the theme a STRUCTURAL input to the ideation specs (03/04/05), not a per-spec optional steer — add a directive that derives the plan's organizing dimensions FROM the theme (the seed + the mission/vision/SLO triad), e.g. a front-load 'derive this plan's organizing dimensions from the theme' step, so a different seed yields a different organizing axis by construction. Bound by grounding-leanness / the stdout cap. Co-resolve with FGAP-030 (guiding_statements into 03/04/05) and DEC-51/DEC-52 (per-actor SLO operationalization + vision/mission as ideation vectors) — the same theme-into-ideation initiative. Investigate the exact directive wording + whether the seed should persist into draft_state for downstream specs, before IMPL.

gaps.title: Prompt-vs-parser citability gap: a spec can render context values (scope_summary/kind) as copy-targets the parser rejects, aborting the run
gaps.package: prompt-workshop spec rendering (snippet 'copy from this list' inventories) vs planner/specs.py parser admission; surfaced at spec 07 (US-LLM-27); design DEC-56
gaps.description: The grounding emits contextual fields (scope_summary, kind, category, prose) beside the citable identity fields (label, statement, code). A spec's prompt can render that superset inside a 'copy exactly from this list' inventory, while the parser admits only the exact identity field at the exact owner/grain. So the model can copy a presented-but-non-admitted value, and the hard catalogue-gate (apply_from_substrate.py parse -> ValueError -> return 1) aborts the whole 42-step run. Spec 07 (propose-responsibilities) is the lone current violator: it renders division/position scope_summary and kind inline with the labels in the citable inventory (snippet lines 26-34), so they read as copyable. The other 13 specs render context but never as copy-targets (spec 13's 'code (category): text', only code cited, is the clean pattern). Three confirming runs aborted at spec 07 on instances of this one class (a scope_summary cited as a division responsibility; a cross-grain atom cited at the position grain).

evidence.reference: renders division/position scope_summary (and kind) inline with the labels inside the citable responsibility inventory the model is told to copy from
_check_in_catalogue + _responsibility_statements_under_division/_under_position admit only the exact responsibility statements at the exact owner/grain; scope_summary and kind are never admitted
parse ValueError -> return 1; a single mis-slotted citation aborts the whole run; a mis-slotted real value is treated identically to a fabrication
the clean exemplar: renders 'code (category): text' and the contract cites only code, so rendered-citable == parser-admissible

gaps.impact: Spec 07 aborts a full workshop generation non-deterministically whenever the model copies a rendered context value; each run can hit a different element of the gap (scope_summary, kind, cross-grain). It blocks every promotion-ready run and the TASK-055 + TASK-056 confirming runs. The hard catalogue-gate kills all 42 steps on one mis-pick.
gaps.proposed_resolution: Per DEC-56: establish the invariant rendered-citable == parser-admissible and enforce it with a deterministic guard across all 14 specs; correct spec 07's render so only the responsibility statements are copy-targets (scope_summary/kind non-citable). No change to the parser, the resolve raise-on-miss, the apply hard-abort, or the DEC-40/DEC-53 gate posture (apply-layer graceful-drop is rejected -- it would breach DEC-40). TASK-057 addresses this gap.
gaps.created_by: orchestrator (2026-06-20; run-surfaced x3, two read-only explores root-caused the class + the apply error model, source-corroborated before filing)

gaps.title: Trio: prose-named WASC standards (A1 narrative) can diverge from the structured accreditation_standards (A2/spec-13); no production prompt or gate reconciles them
gaps.description: Production re-file of the discarded language-polish-pass branch's workshop-scoped FGAP-044, verified live against main 2026-06-21. In the trio, A1 narrative-draft writes prose (current_state/desired_state/rationale/action-step descriptions) that may name WASC standard codes and commit the plan to a targeted set, while A2 propose-accreditation-standards (spec-13) selects the authoritative STRUCTURED accreditation_standards independently from grounding. No production prompt directs the model to keep the prose-named set coherent with the structured set, and _check_promotion_gates only checks accreditation_standards.exists() (non-empty) -- it does NOT verify prose<->structured coherence. So the trio can emit a gate-PASSING draft whose prose commits to standards the structured targeted set omits (or names none the prose advances): an ungated incoherence. Corroborated against current source: no reconcil / 'targeted standards are' / 'coherent with the draft' directive anywhere in ai/migrations (only the no-fabrication preamble); no prose/coherence check in plans/services.py _check_promotion_gates.
gaps.impact: A trio draft can pass the promotion gate while its narrative prose and its structured Standards-Targeted set disagree -- the admin is shown an internally incoherent plan, against NORTH-STAR's 'best route' coherence. Real on the production trio path (A1 + A2 both run). Not a gate blocker (the gate is satisfied by a non-empty structured set), so it surfaces only on read.
gaps.proposed_resolution: A production prompt directive via an ai/00NN guarded migration (canonical pipeline): in the shared preamble and/or the spec-13 body, direct the model to treat the declared structured accreditation_standards as the authoritative targeted set, name a standard in prose only where the plan substantively ties to it (key+name, not an enumerated prose list), and make the proposed structured set coherent with the prose (include every standard the prose substantively advances; propose none the prose gives no support for). The discarded branch's draft directive text (workshop preamble + snippet-13) is a usable starting point. A structural coherence check is a heavier alternative; a prompt directive is the lighter first move.

_check_promotion_gates: grep 'prose|reconcil|named|coheren' returns no prose<->structured coherence check; the gate only asserts accreditation_standards.exists()

gaps.title: DEC-44 fabrication validator resolves its LLM provider without the run's user context (get_llm() no-user), so it silently no-ops under a user-scoped provider config -- the anti-fabrication LLM backstop is OFF whenever the configured provider is not a SYSTEM default
gaps.description: Surfaced by the first real end-to-end run_trio run against the chiway tenant (2026-06-21, --dry-run, openrouter). The trio's spec calls go through get_llm_logged(author) (ai/services/llm/__init__.py:158) which resolves the USER-SPECIFIC LLMProviderConfig first -- the dev DB has exactly one config row: provider=openrouter, user=davidenchine@outlook.com, is_default=True, is_enabled=True, key on row, model=anthropic/claude-sonnet-4.6 -- so the spec calls correctly use openrouter (HTTP 200s observed). But the DEC-44 fabrication validator (planner/_llm_fabrication_validator.py:125) calls get_llm() with NO user, so _get_db_config(user=None) does not match the user-scoped row (an is_default flag on a user-scoped row does not satisfy a no-user lookup), and it falls through to settings (LLM_DEFAULT_PROVIDER unset -> base.py default 'anthropic') with no ANTHROPIC_API_KEY -> NoProviderConfiguredError('No API key configured for anthropic'). The validator is failure-falls-back-without-blocking (planner/_llm_fabrication_validator.py:21,132-134): it logs a WARNING and returns [], so the run PROCEEDS but the DEC-44 LLM fabrication backstop does not fire. Repeated twice in the observed run (validate_candidates invoked per spec that left free-text survivors).

evidence.reference: line 125 `llm = get_llm()` (no user/provider arg); line 50 _DEFAULT_MODEL='anthropic/claude-haiku-4.5'; lines 21,113-134 failure-falls-back-without-blocking -> logs WARNING + returns []

get_llm(user=None) -> _get_db_config(user, provider): user-specific first then settings fallback (LLM_DEFAULT_PROVIDER default 'anthropic', base.py:367); a no-user lookup does not match a user-scoped is_default row

1 LLMProviderConfig: provider=openrouter user=davidenchine@outlook.com is_default=True is_enabled=True key_on_row=True; env LLM_DEFAULT_PROVIDER=None, no OPENROUTER/ANTHROPIC env keys -> run_trio openrouter calls succeed (author path) while the validator's get_llm() resolves anthropic and raises NoProviderConfiguredError
2026-06-21 17:09 run_trio --dry-run: repeated 'DEC-44 validator: provider call failed: NoProviderConfiguredError(...anthropic...)' WARNINGs interleaved with successful openrouter spec calls

gaps.impact: The DEC-44 LLM anti-fabrication backstop (the third-stage validator over the deterministic free-text-audit survivors) silently does not run whenever the active provider is user-scoped or otherwise not a system default -- i.e. in the normal dev configuration. The PRIMARY anti-fabrication protection (the deterministic free-text catalogue audit) still runs, so fabrication is degraded-not-absent; but the LLM backstop that DEC-44 added is effectively off, undetected except by the WARNING log. Affects any get_llm() caller that omits the user when configs are user-scoped, not just the validator.
gaps.proposed_resolution: Thread the run's user (and/or the resolved provider) into the validator so it uses the SAME provider as the invoking run: validate_candidates(... , user=...) -> get_llm(user=...) (the trio passes its author; the dispatch path passes request.user). Needs its own canonical-pipeline pass to trace validate_candidates' callers (audit_freetext / the grounding free-text path) and thread the user through. Consider also: (a) failing LOUDER than a WARNING when the backstop cannot run (it is a safety layer, so a silent no-op is the dangerous part) -- e.g. surface a degraded-validation signal; (b) the anthropic-namespaced _DEFAULT_MODEL is openrouter-compatible (openrouter routes 'anthropic/...'), so the model string is not the bug -- the bug is provider resolution. Operational workaround to make the validator run NOW: make the openrouter LLMProviderConfig a SYSTEM row (user null, is_default) OR set LLM_DEFAULT_PROVIDER=openrouter + OPENROUTER_API_KEY in the env, so get_llm() (no user) resolves openrouter.

gaps.package: school-improvement-plans/planner/orchestration.py (run_trio_to_proposed_plan / the terminal save) + planner/specs.py (the parsers) + planner/views.py (_bind_validate_and_save)
gaps.title: An LLM-produced model field-constraint violation (e.g. label > max_length=200) aborts the WHOLE trio run at the terminal save -- defeating the 'always yields a Plan' + 'no run aborts' guarantees, because constraint violations are not caught until the unguarded terminal save
gaps.description: Surfaced by the second live run_trio run (2026-06-21, chiway, openrouter; after FGAP-050 fixed the validator). The run completed all 14 specs + gate-closure, then aborted with a CommandError aggregating per-formset form-validation errors -- one formset had 4 rows whose `label` was 279-363 chars against the model max_length=200 (Milestone/FeedbackChannel/ReviewEvent/Evidence all define `label = CharField(max_length=200)`: review.py:57, output.py:168, milestone.py:72, feedback.py:48). ROOT CAUSE + abort path (corroborated vs source): the per-spec parsers (planner/specs.py) validate catalogue membership + shape + non-empty, but DO NOT validate model field constraints like max_length (e.g. C1 parse_suggest_feedback_channels:1509 checks label non-empty, not its length). So an over-long label parses + folds + saves into draft_state cleanly. At the terminal save: run_gate_closure_loop probes by saving (save+gate+rollback); an over-long label makes EVERY probe save FAIL form validation -> the loop never converges -> returns plan=None; run_trio_to_proposed_plan's fallback `plan = save_draft_state_as_plan(final_state, ...)` (orchestration.py:1170) then ALSO fails form validation and RAISES -- and that line is NOT wrapped in a try/except -> the ValidationError propagates out of run_trio_to_proposed_plan -> CommandError, NO Plan saved. The resilience loop (_run_spec_with_resilience) only catches PER-SPEC LLM/parse/fold errors (LLMTimeout/RateLimit/ValueError/LLMAuth/_FoldError); it does NOT cover a constraint violation that only manifests at the post-all-specs terminal save. So the DEC-57 dec-4 'no run aborts' + TASK-071's 'always yields a Plan(PROPOSED)' guarantees are BOTH defeated by a single over-long field.

evidence.reference: run_trio_to_proposed_plan:1170 -- `if plan is None: plan = save_draft_state_as_plan(final_state, ...)` is NOT wrapped in try/except; a form-validation ValidationError from the save propagates out -> CommandError, no Plan (the always-yields-a-Plan hole)
C1 parse_suggest_feedback_channels ~1509 (and D1/G1/F5 parsers) validate label non-empty + catalogue, NOT max_length=200 -> over-long values pass parse + fold into draft_state
label = models.CharField(max_length=200) on ReviewEvent:57 / Evidence:168 / Milestone:72 / FeedbackChannel:48 -- the 200-char limit the LLM violated (279-363 chars)
CommandError aggregate: one formset group = [{'label':['...at most 200 characters (it has 306)']}, {...363}, {...279}, {...357}]; all other groups {} (valid)

gaps.impact: A single over-long (or otherwise model-constraint-violating) LLM field aborts the ENTIRE trio run with no Plan persisted -- the exact 'a triviality blocks completion' failure the resilience layer was meant to prevent, but it slips through at the terminal save which is outside the resilience loop. This is a CLASS, not just `label`: any CharField max_length (Plan.title 200, Communication.channel 200, FeedbackChannel.instrument 500, etc.), choices-field value, timing-kind CheckConstraint, or required-FK that a spec fills can equally pass the gate-closure yet fail the terminal save and abort the run. Blocks the post-Phase-15 end-to-end walkthrough (the run cannot produce a draft when any field overflows).
gaps.proposed_resolution: PROPORTIONATE FIX (user-directed 2026-06-21, empirical-first). NO TRUNCATION -- truncation is signal loss that is hard to recover later; rejected. The model field lengths are NOT sacred -- relaxing a capped field (e.g. label -> a larger cap or TextField) is the acceptable FALLBACK if the LLM will not comply, NOT truncation. STEP 1 (do now, then measure): (a) PROMPT -- tell the label/name/title-producing specs, with the REASON, that the value goes into a 200-character database column and WILL BREAK ON SAVE, so produce a concise + communicative label well under 200 chars (the directive itself concise + communicative; a shared-preamble register rule covers all capped name/label/title/channel fields in one edit, landed via a guarded ai/00NN migration). (b) ONE bounded retry-with-feedback: have the label-producing parsers flag an over-long value as a ValueError so the existing resilience loop nudges the LLM once (the rejection text -- N chars, must be <=200 -- becomes the retry steering); bounded, not wasteful. Then DRY-RUN run_trio and OBSERVE whether the prompt+retry makes the LLM comply (it usually will). STEP 2 (only if the dry-run shows the LLM still overflows after the retry): decide between relaxing the field (lengths not sacred) vs another non-lossy handling -- NOT truncation. Note: enabling the parser length-flag converts the current whole-run ABORT (the over-long value otherwise reaches the terminal save and raises) into, at worst, the existing per-spec degrade on retry-exhaustion (the run completes) -- strictly better than abort; the dry-run measures whether exhaustion even occurs. Supersedes the earlier degrade-to-flag and the (rejected) truncate sketches.
